bins.name: Readiness Requirements and Deposits
bins.desc: Switch supports standardized study deposits, readiness requirements, and withdrawal penalties to filter out speculative projects. The company argues the Commission should condition interconnection priority on demonstrated site control, committed customers, permits, executed service agreements, and project financing.
quotes: If the Commission goes forward with the proposed ANOPR, it should focus on expediting interconnection of proven large load projects with demonstrated site control, committed customers, applicable permits, executed service agreements and/or project financing.

This approach is consistent with the fourth principle for reform in the proposed ANOPR about standardized study deposits, readiness requirements, withdrawal penalties and other commitments and financial penalties to deter speculative projects.
Any rules adopted by the Commission should prioritize projects that are ready for interconnection and reduce the incentives for speculative, non-viable projects.

bins.name: Standardized Study Process
bins.desc: Switch supports a uniform, efficient study process with firm timelines for ready projects, modeled on Order 2023 generator interconnection reforms. The company highlights that current regional variation creates uncertainty and delays, and calls for coordination between transmission providers and regional planning entities to eliminate duplicative studies.
quotes: Switch generally supports FERC's effort to develop standardized, transparent, and non-discriminatory procedures tailored to large load interconnections such as data centers.

Current practices vary widely across regions, which can create uncertainty and delays that hinder investment by providing discriminatory access for the interconnection of large loads to the interstate transmission system.

If the Commission goes forward with the proposed ANOPR, it should focus on expediting interconnection of proven large load projects with demonstrated site control, committed customers, applicable permits, executed service agreements and/or project financing.
Any rules adopted by the Commission should prioritize projects that are ready for interconnection and reduce the incentives for speculative, non-viable projects.

As part of this process, the Commission should consider firm timelines for studies and approvals for such projects that are ready for interconnection, similar to generator interconnection reforms under Order No. 2023.
The current process often lacks transparency into load interconnection queues and the study process.
Switch encourages coordination between transmission providers and regional planning entities to avoid duplicative studies and delays.
If the Commission decides to proceed with the proposed ANOPR, it should require transmission providers to publish queue status, study assumptions, and cost estimates for large load interconnections.

bins.name: Non-Discriminatory Transmission Access
bins.desc: Switch frames non-discriminatory access as central to the proceeding, arguing that regional inconsistency currently produces discriminatory outcomes. The company supports a single, consistent framework so all large load applicants face the same process.
quotes: Switch generally supports FERC's effort to develop standardized, transparent, and non-discriminatory procedures tailored to large load interconnections such as data centers.
Timely and non-discriminatory access to the transmission system is essential to reliability, economic growth, and national competitiveness.

Current practices vary widely across regions, which can create uncertainty and delays that hinder investment by providing discriminatory access for the interconnection of large loads to the interstate transmission system.

bins.name: Data Centers Reduce Retail Prices
bins.desc: Switch cites recent research finding that load growth has tended to reduce retail electricity prices by spreading fixed transmission and distribution costs over more users, positioning data center interconnections as a ratepayer benefit.
quotes: Data center development can enhance both the reliability of the grid and affordability for ratepayers by increasing grid infrastructure investment and spreading the fixed costs of the power system over more users.

bins.name: Firm Timelines for Study and Approval
bins.desc: Switch asks the Commission to set mandatory timelines for studies and approvals for projects that meet readiness criteria, drawing an explicit parallel to the timeline provisions in the generator interconnection reforms of Order 2023.
quotes: As part of this process, the Commission should consider firm timelines for studies and approvals for such projects that are ready for interconnection, similar to generator interconnection reforms under Order No. 2023.

bins.name: Queue and Study Transparency
bins.desc: Switch calls for transmission providers to publicly disclose queue status, study assumptions, and cost estimates. The company notes the current process lacks this transparency and that better coordination between transmission providers and regional planners would reduce duplicative work.
quotes: The current process often lacks transparency into load interconnection queues and the study process.
Switch encourages coordination between transmission providers and regional planning entities to avoid duplicative studies and delays.
If the Commission decides to proceed with the proposed ANOPR, it should require transmission providers to publish queue status, study assumptions, and cost estimates for large load interconnections.
